_comment: TEMPLATE (committed, no real machine data). The real per-machine config lives in config/local.json (gitignored) and OVERRIDES these entries. This file just documents the shape and provides a safe 'default' fallback. Keyed by socket.gethostname() -- run 'python run.py config' to see a machine's hostname. 'roots' = estate roots whose CHILD folders are the projects to scan (omit for legacy sibling discovery). Copy an example below into config/local.json, rename the key to your hostname, and fill in real paths.
_roots_comment: 'roots' accepts either a bare path string or {'path': ..., 'scope': 'l5gn'|'mcf'}. The tagged form is how a project gets its registry scope (DECISIONS 0012): scope is declared on the root in the PRODUCER's config, never inferred from folder nesting. That is deliberate -- the layout differs on every machine (the knight has an L5GN folder and no MCF, the gaming rig is flat, the work rig has both), so nesting has never been a reliable signal. Tagging the root gets the same answer with no folder reorg. An untagged root still scans fine; its projects land as scope 'other' and are listed in build_registry --report-aliases under DEPOSIT GAPS.
